name: Trace Labs awesome-osint
description: TraceLabs-endorsed curated list of OSINT frameworks, libraries and software specifically for missing-persons searches.
bestFor: Authoritative starting catalog of missing-persons-relevant OSINT tools curated by Trace Labs.
trustNote: Official Trace Labs curated list; explicitly scoped to missing-persons OSINT.
notes: Source repo for this harvest. README is organized by category (Email, Multi, Username, Social, Phone, People, Images/Video, Documentation/Capture, Geolocation, Domain/IP, Wireless, Translation) and notes it consists of tools that apply specifically to missing-persons searches.

name: Trace Labs OSINT VM (tlosint-vm)
description: Kali-based OSINT Linux distribution pre-loaded with the Trace Labs-endorsed OSINT toolset for CTF and missing-persons investigations.
bestFor: Ready-to-go investigator workstation with curated OSINT tools, Obsidian vault, Tor and OpSec hardening.
trustNote: Official Trace Labs VM build; canonical list of endorsed tools is in scripts/tlosint-tools.sh.
notes: Builds VirtualBox/VMware/OVA images. Provisioning script installs ExifTool, Sherlock, SpiderFoot, PhoneInfoga, Sublist3r, metagoofil, sn0int, Owlculus, steghide/stegseek/stegosuite, translate-shell, Tor/torbrowser, Brave with the Forensic OSINT extension, plus OpSec tools (anonsurf, macchanger, mat2, torsocks). Also ships an Obsidian TL-Vault for case notes.

name: The OSINT Field Manual (TOFM)
description: Trace Labs field manual documenting OSINT methodology and workflows.
bestFor: Reference methodology and tradecraft for running OSINT investigations.
trustNote: Official Trace Labs documentation repo.
notes: Methodology reference rather than a tool; useful as a playbook for missing-persons OSINT.

name: gumshoe
description: Recursive investigation tool based on the OSINT Framework (work in progress).
bestFor: Automating recursive pivots across OSINT Framework resources.
trustNote: Official Trace Labs project (marked WIP).
notes: Go-based, WIP. Wraps the OSINT Framework taxonomy to recursively suggest/investigate sources.

name: h8mail (Trace Labs fork)
description: Email OSINT and breach-hunting tool that searches leaked credentials and breach databases for an email address.
bestFor: Finding breached passwords, accounts and exposures tied to an email address.
trustNote: Trace Labs fork of khast3x/h8mail.
notes: Pip-installable (pip install h8mail). Free local breach files; some integrations (HIBP, Snusbase, etc.) require API keys.

name: Buster
description: Advanced tool for email reconnaissance: finds linked accounts, social profiles and breach data from an email.
bestFor: Pivoting from an email address to associated accounts and profiles.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Email Search).
notes: Aggregates results from multiple sources (Gravatar, HIBP, social APIs). Some lookups benefit from API keys.

name: Have I Been Pwned
description: Check whether an email address or phone number has appeared in known data breaches.
bestFor: Confirming breach exposure and which services an email/phone was registered with.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint; industry-standard breach service by Troy Hunt.
notes: Web lookup is free; API access requires a paid key. Breached-service list helps identify which platforms a subject used.

name: Maltego
description: Link-analysis platform with transforms for OSINT discovery; visualizes relationships between entities as a graph.
bestFor: Visualizing and pivoting across people, emails, domains, phones and social profiles.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Multi Search).
notes: Maltego Community Edition (CE) is free with limited transform results and entity counts; full/commercial editions are paid. Requires account registration.

name: SpiderFoot
description: Automated OSINT reconnaissance framework that collects everything possible about a target across 200+ modules.
bestFor: Hands-off automated enumeration around an email, name, domain, IP or username.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint and installed in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: Open-source self-hosted (web UI on localhost) is free; SpiderFoot HX is a paid hosted version. Many modules actively query the target's infrastructure, so treat as semi-active. Installed from source in the TL VM.

name: WhatsMyName
description: Enumerates a username across hundreds of websites to find where an account exists.
bestFor: Discovering all platforms where a target's username is registered.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Username Search); WhatsMyName project by WebBreacher.
notes: Web app and underlying wmn-data.json are free; data set powers many other tools. No login required.

name: Sherlock
description: Command-line tool that hunts a username across 400+ social networks.
bestFor: Fast CLI enumeration of a username across social platforms.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint and installed in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: Installed via pipx in the TL VM. Outputs found profile URLs; good first pivot from a username.

name: Forensic OSINT Full Page Screen Capture
description: Chrome/Brave extension that captures full-page screenshots and video for forensically-sound evidence collection.
bestFor: Preserving web pages and social profiles as defensible evidence during an investigation.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint and force-installed into Brave by the TL VM.
notes: TL VM force-installs this extension into Brave (install_brave_forced_extension_forensic_osint). Free tier captures pages; advanced/forensic features are paid.

name: Instaloader
description: Downloads Instagram photos, videos, captions, comments and metadata for a profile or hashtag.
bestFor: Bulk-archiving a subject's Instagram content and metadata.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Social Media).
notes: Authenticated use risks Instagram rate-limiting/bans; use a burner account. Captures post timestamps, captions and tagged users useful for timelines and associates.

name: PhoneInfoga
description: Information-gathering framework for international phone numbers (carrier, line type, footprint, OSINT links).
bestFor: Validating a phone number and pivoting to carrier/region and online footprint.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint and installed in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: Installed via Go/official script in the TL VM (also offers a web/local server UI). Some scanners (e.g. Numverify) need free API keys.

name: Truecaller
description: Caller ID and spam-lookup service that can resolve a phone number to a likely name.
bestFor: Resolving an unknown phone number to a name or business.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Phone Numbers); noted as commercial.
notes: Free tier (mobile app / limited web search) available with registration; Premium is paid. OpSec: searching exposes your account; use carefully.

name: Google Images
description: Reverse image search to find where an image appears online.
bestFor: Finding the source/other appearances of a photo of a person or place.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Images & Video).
notes: Weaker on faces than Yandex; strong on objects, logos and landmarks for geolocation.

name: Yandex Images
description: Reverse image search with notably strong face-matching capabilities.
bestFor: Reverse-searching faces and identifying people from photos.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint; widely regarded best free reverse face search.
notes: Best free option for face matching; strongest coverage for Eastern Europe/Russia. Crop to the face for better matches.

name: Surfface
description: Face-search and people finder that indexes social profiles and public media by face.
bestFor: Finding social profiles and public photos matching a face.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Images & Video Analysis).
notes: Face-recognition search engine; free searches typically limited, with paid tiers for more results. Verify legality/ToS before use.

name: ExifTool
description: Reads and writes metadata (EXIF, GPS, timestamps, device info) in images, video and documents.
bestFor: Extracting GPS coordinates, timestamps and camera/device data from media.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint and installed in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: Core forensic utility; GPS tags can directly geolocate where a photo was taken. Installed as an apt package in the TL VM.

name: Jimpl EXIF Viewer
description: Simple online viewer for image metadata (EXIF/GPS) with no install required.
bestFor: Quick web-based check of a photo's EXIF/GPS when CLI isn't available.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Images & Video).
notes: Uploading a photo to a third-party site has OpSec implications; prefer ExifTool locally for sensitive evidence.

name: FFmpeg
description: Multimedia framework for extracting, converting and processing video and audio.
bestFor: Pulling frames/thumbnails and metadata from video evidence for analysis.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Images & Video Analysis).
notes: Use to extract key frames from a video for reverse image/face search, or to read embedded video metadata.

name: GoWitness
description: CLI tool that takes screenshots of web pages headlessly for evidence collection.
bestFor: Bulk-screenshotting URLs/profiles to preserve them as evidence.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Documentation & Capture); by SensePost.
notes: Actively requests each page (leaves your IP in target logs). Great for capturing many profile URLs at once.

name: Wayback Machine
description: Browse historical snapshots of websites and deleted/changed pages.
bestFor: Recovering deleted social posts, profiles or pages tied to a subject.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Documentation & Capture); Internet Archive.
notes: Passive (queries the archive, not the target). Essential for recovering content a subject has since removed.

name: Google Maps
description: Maps with Street View and satellite imagery for location verification and geolocation.
bestFor: Verifying addresses and geolocating photos via Street View landmarks.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Geolocation & Mapping).
notes: Street View and satellite layers are the workhorses for matching photo backgrounds to real locations.

name: Mapillary
description: Crowdsourced street-level imagery, useful where Google Street View has no coverage.
bestFor: Street-level geolocation in areas Google Street View doesn't cover.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Geolocation & Mapping).
notes: Owned by Meta; free imagery and API. Complements Google Street View for rural/non-Western areas.

name: Shodan
description: Search engine for internet-connected devices and services.
bestFor: Mapping a subject's exposed devices/services from an IP or domain.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint; CLI installed in the TL VM.
notes: Free account gives limited searches; paid plans unlock more. Mostly infra/CTI relevance, occasionally useful for webcams/devices tied to a location.

name: Censys
description: Internet-wide scanning and intelligence platform for hosts, certificates and domains.
bestFor: Investigating infrastructure, certificates and exposed services for a domain/IP.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Domain/IP/Infrastructure).
notes: Free account with query limits; paid for heavier use. Primarily infra/CTI, low direct relevance to finding a person.

name: WiGLE
description: Crowdsourced database mapping wireless networks (SSIDs/BSSIDs) to geographic locations.
bestFor: Geolocating a Wi-Fi SSID/BSSID or finding networks near a location.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Wireless).
notes: Free with a registered account and rate limits. Can place a known SSID/BSSID (e.g. from a photo of a router or device) on a map.

name: Translate Shell
description: Command-line translator backed by Google, Bing, Yandex and other engines.
bestFor: Quickly translating foreign-language posts, names and content from the terminal.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint and installed in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: apt package 'translate-shell'; invoked as 'trans'. Useful when a subject posts in another language.

name: DeepL
description: High-quality machine translation with strong European and Asian language support.
bestFor: Accurate translation of nuanced foreign-language content.
trustNote: Listed in Trace Labs awesome-osint (Translation).
notes: Free web tier with character limits; DeepL Pro/API are paid. Generally higher quality than Google Translate for European languages.

name: Sublist3r
description: Enumerates subdomains of a domain using OSINT sources and search engines.
bestFor: Mapping a target organization's or person's web infrastructure via subdomains.
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM (install_sublist3r).
notes: Installed via pipx in the TL VM. Infrastructure recon; low direct relevance to locating an individual.

name: metagoofil
description: Extracts metadata from public documents (PDF, DOC, XLS) found on a target domain.
bestFor: Harvesting author names, usernames and software from a target's public documents.
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM (install_metagoofil).
notes: Installed via pipx in the TL VM. Document metadata often leaks real names, usernames and internal paths.

name: sn0int
description: Semi-automatic OSINT framework and package manager for scriptable reconnaissance modules.
bestFor: Scriptable, modular OSINT enumeration across domains, emails, usernames and more.
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM (install_sn0int, kpcyrd apt repo).
notes: Installed via Cargo/kpcyrd apt repo in the TL VM. Module registry includes social, geo and domain modules useful for pivoting.

name: Owlculus
description: OSINT case-management and investigation toolkit with a web UI for organizing entities and evidence.
bestFor: Managing a missing-persons case: tracking entities, evidence and running OSINT plugins.
trustNote: Installed (Docker-based) in the Trace Labs VM (install_owlculus).
notes: TL VM clones it to /opt/owlculus and starts a Docker stack; open the web UI manually. Combines case management with built-in OSINT plugins.

name: steghide
description: Steganography tool to detect and extract data hidden inside image and audio files.
bestFor: Recovering hidden/embedded data from images shared by a subject (CTF-style).
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM (install_steghide / apt steghide).
notes: apt package 'steghide'. Mostly CTF/forensics relevance; occasionally surfaces hidden notes in shared media.

name: stegseek
description: Fast steghide cracker that brute-forces passphrases to extract hidden data.
bestFor: Cracking steghide-protected files when the passphrase is unknown.
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM (install_stegseek).
notes: Companion to steghide; primarily CTF/forensics use.

name: StegOSuite
description: Graphical steganography tool to hide and extract data in image files.
bestFor: GUI-based detection/extraction of hidden data in images.
trustNote: Optionally installed in the Trace Labs VM (install_stegosuite).
notes: Java GUI; marked optional in the TL VM build. CTF/forensics relevance.

name: Brave Browser
description: Privacy-focused Chromium browser shipped in the Trace Labs VM with OSINT extensions pre-installed.
bestFor: Privacy-respecting browsing for investigations with the Forensic OSINT capture extension.
trustNote: Installed and configured (with forced Forensic OSINT extension) in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: TL VM installs Brave via its apt repo and force-installs the Forensic OSINT capture extension. Good base browser for sock-puppet research.

name: Tor / Tor Browser
description: Anonymity network and hardened browser for OpSec-conscious browsing and dark-web access.
bestFor: Anonymizing investigator traffic and reaching .onion resources.
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM (apt tor, torbrowser-launcher, torsocks).
notes: TL VM installs tor, torbrowser-launcher and torsocks. Core OpSec layer to avoid leaking investigator identity.

name: Kali Anonsurf
description: Routes all system traffic through Tor and provides anti-forensic helpers for anonymity.
bestFor: System-wide anonymization of investigator network traffic.
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM (apt anonsurf).
notes: OpSec tooling; routes traffic through Tor at the OS level. Supports investigator anonymity rather than finding a person.

name: macchanger
description: Utility to view and spoof the MAC address of network interfaces.
bestFor: Randomizing the investigator's hardware MAC for OpSec.
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM (apt macchanger).
notes: Pure OpSec utility installed by the TL VM.

name: mat2 (Metadata Anonymisation Toolkit)
description: Strips metadata from files (images, PDFs, office docs) before sharing.
bestFor: Scrubbing metadata from investigator-produced files before release.
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM (apt mat2).
notes: OpSec hygiene tool; complements ExifTool for cleaning rather than reading metadata.

name: Obsidian (TL-Vault)
description: Markdown knowledge-base app shipped with a Trace Labs case-notes vault (TL-Vault) and plugins.
bestFor: Organizing case notes, entities and findings during an investigation.
trustNote: Installed in the Trace Labs VM with a pre-built TL-Vault (Dataview, Kanban, QuickAdd plugins).
notes: TL VM ships a TL-Vault on the desktop with Dataview/Kanban/QuickAdd. Free for personal use; commercial use needs a license. Companion repo: tracelabs/Trace-Labs-Obsidian-Theme.

name: OSINT Framework
description: Web directory that organizes OSINT resources by data type and investigation goal.
bestFor: Browsing categorized OSINT resources by selector (email, username, phone, etc.).
trustNote: Referenced/bookmarked in the Trace Labs VM and underpins the gumshoe tool.
notes: Bookmarked in the TL VM; the taxonomy that the TL 'gumshoe' tool is built around. Strong jumping-off point per data type.

name: crt.sh
description: Certificate Transparency log search to find subdomains and hosts via issued TLS certificates.
bestFor: Discovering subdomains/hosts tied to a domain via CT logs.
trustNote: Bookmarked in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: Infrastructure recon; low direct relevance to locating a person.

name: urlscan.io
description: Scans and screenshots URLs, recording domains, IPs and resources contacted.
bestFor: Safely inspecting a suspicious URL and its infrastructure.
trustNote: Bookmarked in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: Free with public scans; private scans/API need an account. Mostly CTI relevance.

name: GreyNoise Visualizer
description: Shows internet-scanning context for an IP address (benign vs malicious noise).
bestFor: Contextualizing an IP address's internet behavior.
trustNote: Bookmarked in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: CTI/infra relevance; not directly useful for finding a person.

name: VirusTotal
description: Aggregated file/URL/domain/IP reputation and relationship analysis.
bestFor: Checking files, domains and IPs and pivoting on related artifacts.
trustNote: Bookmarked in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: Free web search; API limited on free tier. Primarily malware/CTI; low missing-persons relevance.

name: who.is
description: WHOIS lookup for domain registration and ownership details.
bestFor: Finding registrant name/email/org behind a domain.
trustNote: Bookmarked in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: Registrant data is often privacy-protected now, but historical/unprotected records can reveal a real name, email or address.

name: BuiltWith
description: Identifies the technologies, trackers and analytics IDs a website uses.
bestFor: Linking sites by shared analytics/tracker IDs to a common owner.
trustNote: Bookmarked in the Trace Labs VM.
notes: Shared Google Analytics/AdSense IDs across sites can connect a person's multiple web properties. Free basic lookups; deeper data is paid.
